text_segment: An ecosystem includes all living things in an area plus the nonliving things around them. Plants, animals, water, soil, and sunlight all work together. Each part of an ecosystem depends on the others.
question: Explain what makes up an ecosystem.
Active: Ecosystems include both living and nonliving things.
Progressive: Living and nonliving things are working together.
Passive: Both living and nonliving things are included in ecosystems.
Subordinate: Because everything is connected, ecosystems need balance.
order_passive_beginner_1: both living and nonliving things,are included,in,ecosystems,.

text_segment: A population is a group of the same species living in one area. All the deer in a forest make up one population. All the ants in your backyard are another population.
question: Describe what a population means in science.
Active: Populations contain organisms of the same species.
Progressive: Scientists are counting organisms in a population.
Passive: Organisms of one species are grouped into populations.
Subordinate: When organisms share an area, they form a population.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: when,organisms share an area,,,they form,a population,.

text_segment: A community is made up of different species living together in one place. In a pond community, you might find fish, frogs, insects, and plants. All these different species interact with each other.
question: Explain what a community means in ecology.
Active: Communities contain many different species together.
Progressive: Different species are living together in communities.
Passive: Many species are found living in communities.
Subordinate: Because species interact, they form communities.

text_segment: The biosphere is the part of Earth where life exists. It includes land, water, and air where living things are found. Earth is the only biosphere we know of in the universe.
question: Describe what the biosphere is.
Active: The biosphere contains all life on Earth.
Progressive: Life is existing throughout the entire biosphere.
Passive: All life on Earth is contained in the biosphere.
Subordinate: Because Earth supports life, it forms a biosphere.
order_passive_beginner_1: all life on earth,is contained,in the,biosphere,.

text_segment: A habitat is the natural home of a plant or animal. It provides food, water, shelter, and space to live. Different species need different types of habitats to survive.
question: Explain what a habitat provides for animals.
Active: Habitats provide food, water, and shelter for animals.
Progressive: Habitats are providing everything animals need.
Passive: Food and shelter are provided by habitats.
Subordinate: When habitats are healthy, animals can thrive.

text_segment: A niche is the role an animal or plant plays in its ecosystem. Bees have the niche of pollinating flowers. Worms have the niche of breaking down dead plants in soil.
question: Describe what an ecological niche means.
Active: Each species fills a special role called a niche.
Progressive: Bees are filling their niche by pollinating flowers.
Passive: A special role is filled by each species.
Subordinate: Because bees pollinate flowers, they fill an important niche.
order_active_beginner_1: each species,fills a special role,called a,niche,.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,bees pollinate flowers,,,they fill,an important niche,.

text_segment: Symbiosis is when two different species live closely together. In mutualism, both species benefit. Clownfish live in sea anemones, and both are protected from predators.
question: Explain what mutualism means.
Active: Mutualism helps both species in the relationship.
Progressive: Both species are benefiting from mutualism.
Passive: Both species are helped by mutualistic relationships.
Subordinate: When both species benefit, the relationship is mutualism.

text_segment: In parasitism, one species benefits while harming another. Ticks attach to animals and feed on their blood. The tick benefits, but the host animal is harmed.
question: Describe what parasitism means.
Active: Parasites harm their hosts while benefiting themselves.
Progressive: Ticks are harming animals by feeding on blood.
Passive: Host animals are harmed by parasites.
Subordinate: Because ticks feed on blood, they are parasites.

text_segment: A food chain shows how energy moves from one living thing to another. Plants make food from sunlight. Animals eat plants, and other animals eat those animals.
question: Explain how a food chain works.
Active: Food chains show how energy moves between organisms.
Progressive: Energy is moving through the food chain.
Passive: Energy is passed along through food chains.
Subordinate: When animals eat plants, energy moves up the food chain.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: when,animals eat plants,,,energy moves up,the food chain,.

text_segment: Producers are living things that make their own food. Plants use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis. Without producers, no other life could exist.
question: Describe what producers do in an ecosystem.
Active: Producers make their own food using sunlight.
Progressive: Plants are making food through photosynthesis.
Passive: Food is made by producers using sunlight.
Subordinate: Because plants make food, they are called producers.

text_segment: Consumers are animals that eat other living things. Herbivores eat plants. Carnivores eat other animals. Omnivores eat both plants and animals.
question: Explain what consumers are in a food chain.
Active: Consumers eat plants or other animals for energy.
Progressive: Animals are consuming plants and other animals.
Passive: Plants and animals are eaten by consumers.
Subordinate: Because animals eat other organisms, they are consumers.

text_segment: Decomposers break down dead plants and animals. Fungi, bacteria, and some insects are decomposers. They return nutrients to the soil so new plants can grow.
question: Describe what decomposers do.
Active: Decomposers break down dead matter into nutrients.
Progressive: Fungi are breaking down fallen leaves.
Passive: Dead matter is broken down by decomposers.
Subordinate: After organisms die, decomposers return nutrients to soil.

text_segment: Biodiversity means having many different species in an area. Healthy ecosystems have high biodiversity. When biodiversity decreases, ecosystems become weaker and less stable.
question: Explain why biodiversity is important.
Active: Biodiversity makes ecosystems stronger and healthier.
Progressive: Many species are living together in healthy ecosystems.
Passive: Ecosystems are made stronger by high biodiversity.
Subordinate: When many species exist together, biodiversity is high.

text_segment: Conservation means protecting natural resources and wildlife. We conserve by using less water, recycling, and protecting habitats. Conservation helps keep our planet healthy for the future.
question: Describe what conservation means.
Active: Conservation protects natural resources for the future.
Progressive: People are conserving resources by recycling.
Passive: Natural resources are protected through conservation.
Subordinate: By using less water, we practice conservation.

text_segment: Endangered species are animals or plants that may soon go extinct. Their populations have become very small. Losing habitat and pollution are common reasons species become endangered.
question: Explain what makes a species endangered.
Active: Endangered species face the risk of extinction.
Progressive: Many species are becoming endangered due to habitat loss.
Passive: Species are endangered when populations become very small.
Subordinate: When habitats are destroyed, species become endangered.
order_progressive_beginner_1: many species,are becoming endangered,due to,habitat loss,.

text_segment: Extinction happens when the last member of a species dies. Once extinct, a species is gone forever. Dinosaurs and dodo birds are examples of extinct animals.
question: Describe what extinction means.
Active: Extinction means a species disappears forever.
Progressive: Species are going extinct at an alarming rate.
Passive: Species are lost forever through extinction.
Subordinate: After the last member dies, a species becomes extinct.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: after,the last member dies,,,a species,becomes extinct,.

text_segment: Air pollution happens when harmful gases and particles enter the atmosphere. Cars, factories, and power plants release pollutants. Air pollution can cause breathing problems and harm the environment.
question: Explain what causes air pollution.
Active: Cars and factories release pollutants into the air.
Progressive: Factories are releasing harmful gases daily.
Passive: Pollutants are released into the air by vehicles.
Subordinate: When cars burn fuel, they create air pollution.

text_segment: Acid rain forms when pollution mixes with water in clouds. It damages forests, kills fish in lakes, and harms buildings. Reducing factory emissions helps prevent acid rain.
question: Describe how acid rain affects the environment.
Active: Acid rain damages forests and kills fish.
Progressive: Acid rain is harming trees and lakes.
Passive: Forests and fish are damaged by acid rain.
Subordinate: When pollution mixes with clouds, acid rain forms.

text_segment: Water pollution happens when harmful substances enter rivers, lakes, and oceans. Garbage, chemicals, and oil spills cause water pollution. Polluted water harms fish and other aquatic life.
question: Explain what causes water pollution.
Active: Chemicals and garbage pollute our waterways.
Progressive: Pollution is entering rivers and lakes daily.
Passive: Rivers and lakes are polluted by harmful substances.
Subordinate: When oil spills occur, water becomes polluted.

text_segment: Biomagnification happens when pollutants build up in the food chain. Small fish eat polluted algae. Bigger fish eat many small fish and get more pollution in their bodies.
question: Describe how biomagnification works.
Active: Pollutants increase as they move up the food chain.
Progressive: Toxins are building up in larger predators.
Passive: Toxins are concentrated through biomagnification.
Subordinate: Because bigger fish eat many smaller fish, toxins increase.
tiles_subordinate: bigger fish eat many smaller fish
order_active_beginner_1: pollutants,increase as they move,up the,food chain,.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,bigger fish eat many smaller fish,,,toxins,increase,.

text_segment: Land pollution occurs when trash and chemicals contaminate soil. Landfills, illegal dumping, and pesticides cause land pollution. Polluted soil cannot grow healthy plants.
question: Explain what causes land pollution.
Active: Trash and chemicals contaminate the soil.
Progressive: Landfills are filling up with garbage.
Passive: Soil is contaminated by chemicals and trash.
Subordinate: When chemicals leak into soil, land pollution occurs.

text_segment: Erosion happens when wind, water, or ice wear away soil and rock. Heavy rains can wash soil into rivers. Planting trees and grasses helps prevent erosion.
question: Describe what causes erosion.
Active: Water and wind wear away soil over time.
Progressive: Rain is washing soil into the river.
Passive: Soil is worn away by wind and water.
Subordinate: When heavy rains fall, erosion carries soil away.

text_segment: The ozone layer protects Earth from harmful sun rays. It acts like a shield in the atmosphere. Certain chemicals once damaged the ozone, but we have reduced their use.
question: Explain what the ozone layer does.
Active: The ozone layer protects us from harmful rays.
Progressive: The ozone layer is shielding Earth from radiation.
Passive: Earth is protected by the ozone layer.
Subordinate: Because ozone blocks harmful rays, life can exist.

text_segment: A watershed is an area of land where all water drains to the same place. Rain falling on hills flows into streams, then rivers, then larger bodies of water. Protecting watersheds keeps our drinking water clean.
question: Describe what a watershed is.
Active: Watersheds collect water and drain it to one place.
Progressive: Rain is flowing through the watershed into rivers.
Passive: Water is collected and drained by watersheds.
Subordinate: When rain falls on hills, it flows into watersheds.
order_active_beginner_1: watersheds,collect water,and drain it,to one place,.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: when,rain falls on hills,,,it flows,into watersheds,.

text_segment: The greenhouse effect traps heat in Earth's atmosphere. Gases like carbon dioxide act like a blanket. Too much of these gases causes global warming and climate change.
question: Explain what the greenhouse effect does.
Active: Greenhouse gases trap heat in the atmosphere.
Progressive: Carbon dioxide is trapping more heat than before.
Passive: Heat is trapped by greenhouse gases.
Subordinate: When greenhouse gases increase, temperatures rise.

text_segment: Reduce, reuse, and recycle are the three R's of conservation. Reducing means buying less. Reusing means using things again. Recycling turns old materials into new products.
question: Describe the three R's of conservation.
Active: The three R's help reduce waste and pollution.
Progressive: People are recycling to help the environment.
Passive: Waste is reduced by following the three R's.
Subordinate: By recycling materials, we create less pollution.

text_segment: Invasive species are plants or animals that spread where they do not belong. They often have no natural predators in new areas. Invasive species crowd out native plants and animals.
question: Explain why invasive species are harmful.
Active: Invasive species harm native plants and animals.
Progressive: Invasive plants are spreading across the forest.
Passive: Native species are harmed by invasive organisms.
Subordinate: Because they have no predators, invasive species spread fast.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,they have no predators,,,invasive species,spread fast,.

text_segment: Pollinators like bees, butterflies, and birds help plants reproduce. They carry pollen from flower to flower. Without pollinators, many plants could not make seeds or fruit.
question: Describe why pollinators are important.
Active: Pollinators help plants make seeds and fruit.
Progressive: Bees are carrying pollen between flowers.
Passive: Pollen is carried between flowers by pollinators.
Subordinate: Without bees and butterflies, many plants cannot reproduce.

text_segment: Hybrid vehicles use both gasoline and electricity for power. They use less fuel than regular cars. Driving hybrid vehicles helps reduce air pollution.
question: Explain how hybrid vehicles help the environment.
Active: Hybrid vehicles use less fuel and reduce pollution.
Progressive: Hybrid cars are using both gas and electricity.
Passive: Less fuel is used by hybrid vehicles.
Subordinate: Because hybrids use less gas, they create less pollution.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,hybrids use less gas,,,they create,less pollution,.

text_segment: Renewable energy comes from sources that never run out. Solar power comes from sunlight. Wind power comes from moving air. These clean energy sources do not cause pollution.
question: Describe what renewable energy is.
Active: Renewable energy comes from sources that never run out.
Progressive: Solar panels are collecting energy from sunlight.
Passive: Clean energy is produced by wind and solar power.
Subordinate: Because sunlight is endless, solar energy is renewable.
order_active_beginner_1: renewable energy,comes from sources,that never,run out,.
order_passive_beginner_1: clean energy,is produced,by wind,and solar power,.

text_segment: Nonpoint source pollution comes from many places spread over a large area. Rainwater washes fertilizers, oil, and trash into streams. This type of pollution is hard to trace to one source.
question: Explain what nonpoint source pollution means.
Active: Nonpoint pollution comes from many scattered sources.
Progressive: Rainwater is carrying pollutants into streams.
Passive: Pollutants are washed into streams by rainwater.
Subordinate: Because pollution comes from everywhere, it is hard to stop.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,pollution comes from everywhere,,,it is hard,to stop,.

text_segment: A brownfield is land that was used before but may now be polluted. Old factories and gas stations often leave behind contaminated soil. Brownfields must be cleaned before new buildings can be built.
question: Describe what a brownfield is.
Active: Brownfields are polluted lands from old factories.
Progressive: Workers are cleaning up the old brownfield site.
Passive: Contaminated land is called a brownfield.
Subordinate: Before building on brownfields, the soil must be cleaned.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: before,building on brownfields,,,the soil,must be cleaned,.

text_segment: National parks protect natural areas for everyone to enjoy. The National Park Service was created in 1916. These parks preserve wildlife, forests, and beautiful landscapes for future generations.
question: Explain why national parks are important.
Active: National parks protect nature for future generations.
Progressive: Rangers are protecting wildlife in the parks.
Passive: Natural areas are protected by national parks.
Subordinate: Because parks preserve nature, wildlife can thrive.

text_segment: Earth Day is celebrated on April 22 each year. It started in 1970 to raise awareness about the environment. People plant trees, clean up litter, and learn about protecting our planet.
question: Describe what Earth Day celebrates.
Active: Earth Day celebrates and protects our planet.
Progressive: People are planting trees on Earth Day.
Passive: Environmental awareness is raised on Earth Day.
Subordinate: Since 1970, Earth Day has helped protect the planet.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: since,1970,,,earth day has,helped protect the planet,.

text_segment: The EPA is the Environmental Protection Agency. It was created in 1970 to protect human health and the environment. The EPA makes rules about air and water quality.
question: Explain what the EPA does.
Active: The EPA protects human health and the environment.
Progressive: The EPA is setting rules for clean air.
Passive: Environmental rules are made by the EPA.
Subordinate: Since 1970, the EPA has protected the environment.

text_segment: An environmental impact statement explains how a project might affect nature. Before building, people must study the effects on air, water, soil, and wildlife. This helps protect the environment from harm.
question: Describe what an environmental impact statement does.
Active: Impact statements study how projects affect nature.
Progressive: Scientists are studying the project's effects.
Passive: Environmental effects are studied before building.
Subordinate: Before construction begins, impacts must be studied.

text_segment: Fuel cells make electricity from hydrogen and oxygen. They produce only water as waste. Fuel cells are a clean energy source that does not pollute the air.
question: Explain how fuel cells help the environment.
Active: Fuel cells produce clean energy without pollution.
Progressive: Fuel cells are producing electricity from hydrogen.
Passive: Clean electricity is produced by fuel cells.
Subordinate: Because fuel cells only release water, they are clean.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,fuel cells only release water,,,they are,clean,.

text_segment: Leave No Trace means leaving nature as you found it. Pack out all your trash. Stay on trails and do not disturb wildlife. These rules help protect wild places.
question: Describe what Leave No Trace means.
Active: Leave No Trace protects nature from human damage.
Progressive: Hikers are packing out all their trash.
Passive: Nature is protected by following Leave No Trace.
Subordinate: When hiking, pack out everything you bring in.

text_segment: Some endangered species have recovered thanks to conservation. Bald eagles were nearly extinct but are now common again. Protection laws and habitat restoration helped save them.
question: Explain how endangered species can recover.
Active: Protection and habitat restoration save species.
Progressive: Bald eagle populations are recovering quickly.
Passive: Species are saved by conservation efforts.
Subordinate: After protection laws passed, bald eagles recovered.

text_segment: Environmental scientists study nature and find ways to protect it. They test water and air for pollution. Other careers include park rangers, wildlife biologists, and environmental engineers.
question: Describe careers in environmental science.
Active: Environmental scientists study and protect nature.
Progressive: Scientists are testing water quality for pollution.
Passive: Nature is studied and protected by scientists.
Subordinate: If you love nature, environmental science offers many careers.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: if,you love nature,,,environmental science,offers many careers,.
